description: End-to-end QUIC transport tuning benchmark (Track J7)

quic_tuning_small_message/default_config.description: 100-byte round-trip with default config (Cubic, 10ms RTT, 5ms ACK)
quic_tuning_small_message/default_config.note: Includes full TLS handshake per iteration (no connection reuse)

quic_tuning_small_message/low_latency_bb.description: 100-byte round-trip with low-latency config (BBR, 10ms RTT, 5ms ACK, 1MB window)
quic_tuning_small_message/low_latency_bb.note: BBR shows slight improvement over Cubic for small messages

quic_tuning_small_message/newreno_config.note: NewReno is fastest for localhost (no congestion to manage)

quic_tuning_stream_open/low_latency_bbr.description: Stream open latency on existing connection (BBR)
quic_tuning_stream_open/low_latency_bbr.note: 1.34µs to open a bidirectional stream — transport tuning is working

key_findings.stream_open_latency: 1.34µs — extremely fast stream open on tuned transport
key_findings.bbr_vs_cubic: BBR shows ~4% improvement over Cubic for small messages (435µs vs 454µs)
key_findings.newreno_localhost: NewReno is fastest on localhost (368µs) — no congestion to manage
key_findings.note: Round-trip times are dominated by TLS handshake. For pooled connections, the per-RPC cost is just the stream open time (1.34µs).

methodology.note: Each iteration creates a new connection (full TLS handshake). The stream_open benchmark isolates transport tuning from TLS.

j6_skipped: Multi-path QUIC skipped — quinn doesn't support multi-path natively. Would require switching to quiche or s2n-quic.
